candidates.rationale: Selected target. CodeClash supplies goal-oriented multi-round software development with logs and public ELO anchors; SWE-bench Verified supplies human-validated patch tasks and a stable coding-agent baseline. The Telos overlay adds completion receipts, diff scope, verification claims, and stop-correctness without inventing a private benchmark.

Strong public coding-agent anchor with 500 human-validated instances, patch artifacts, and test-based scoring. Not selected alone because leaderboard saturation and ticket-shaped tasks make the core Telos question narrower than the mission: goal completion can still be proxied by visible tests.

High-value later line: direct AI R&D capability with human comparisons and realistic research engineering. Not first because the frozen gate caps operational cost at 3; RE-Bench is better after the receipt protocol is proven on a cheaper software target.

Good service-agent target because policy adherence, tool calls, user interaction, and database state are naturally receipt-bearing. Not first because the public tau-bench repository warns its original tasks are outdated and the current tau2/tau3 line adds heavier setup and voice/knowledge complexity.

Useful later security line because it measures tool-agent behavior under adversarial prompt-injection pressure. Not first because it would make Telos look like a prompt-injection benchmark instead of a general completion-proof protocol.
